theme: The Honest Sailor and the Drowning Coast
text:
An honest sailor named Milo took payment in milk and bread when coin was scarce, and he never grew tired of the sea. The void of the open water did not frighten him, though pirates once tried to decorate their flag with his blood. Sailing northward past an old monastery, he watched the tide inundate the lowlands while a lone hunter waded after game.

Milo was every captain's favorite customer at the harbor market, where merchants treated him kindly. He kept a modest wardrobe in a cabin, his countless shirts folded with one bruised elbow resting on the rail as he liked to compose songs. The town would celebrate his return, though some, in disgust, recalled the formation of his temper after long voyages. An overload of work could break a weaker man.

Near the lavatory of the old inn, leftover stew waited. A revolutionary mood gripped the reality of the docks that season: tension ran high. A traveling carnivore-keeper let his beasts peck and prowl, and Milo's conviction held firm. A bad joint in the pier made him dread the next storm, yet it was expedient to stay. Conditions were conducive to disaster. Sailors stored dynamite to proclaim the festival, and a great tanker, owing money to half the coast, threatened to beset the bay.

To synchronize the rescue boats was hard, for weakness in the crew was important to hide. Had he ever soared so close to ruin? When the flood came to submerge the lower streets, the event swept them all. Milo led them to higher ground; the waterfront could not hamper him. The facility for calm under pressure is rare. Men die when they panic, and pollution from the spilled tomato cannery only worsened the inverse logic of the chaos.

Registration of the missing took a week. On his way home, confident again, Milo passed a radish field where the salt had begun to tarnish the soil. He would not obsess over loss. Culture, he believed, is the whole of how people utter their grief, whereas a rascal hides it. A peculiarity of his communal village was that no one stayed helpless long; one contingency met another.

A single spark in a narrow alley nearly undid the recovery, but they managed to pack and level the debris, pin by pin. Commerce returned. The harbor began to attract trade again. Amid the lotus ponds, in a lucid evening, the navy raised a flag for those lost to the extinct old ways. Each denomination of grief found its symbol. A single pirate, more conspicuous than any disturbance the storm had caused, tried to force the survivors to give consent to a looting scheme, but Milo, adept at reading wicked men, refused. Only those of proven honesty were eligible to share the salvage. Watching the gulls soar above the calm water, he felt at peace. Milo, ever communicative, helped design the new packaging for the rescued goods, and chose to innovate the warning system so no town would again be caught upside down by the sea.
translation:
一个名叫米洛的诚实水手,在钱币稀缺时以牛奶和面包作为报酬,而他从不厌倦大海。开阔水域的空旷并不令他害怕,尽管海盗曾试图用他的血来装点他们的旗帜。向北航行经过一座古老的修道院时,他看着潮水淹没低地,一名孤独的猎人正涉水追逐猎物。

在港口市场,米洛是每位船长最喜欢的顾客,商人们待他和善。他在船舱里有一个简朴的衣橱,无数件衬衫叠放着,他喜欢一只擦伤的胳膊肘靠在栏杆上谱写歌曲。小镇会庆祝他的归来,尽管有些人厌恶地回忆起他长途航行后脾气的形成。过量的工作会击垮意志薄弱的人。

在那间老客栈的盥洗室附近,有剩下的炖菜在等着。那个季节,码头的现实被一种革命性的情绪攫住:紧张气氛高涨。一个流动的食肉动物饲养者让他的野兽啄食、徘徊,而米洛的信念依然坚定。码头上一处糟糕的接缝让他畏惧下一场风暴,然而留下来是权宜之计。条件有利于灾难发生。水手们储存炸药以宣告节日的到来,而一艘欠了半个海岸钱的大油轮,威胁要围困海湾。

要使救援船同步很难,因为船员的弱点需要重要地隐藏起来。他可曾如此接近毁灭?当洪水前来淹没下面的街道时,这场事件席卷了他们所有人。米洛带他们到高地;滨水区无法阻碍他。在压力下保持冷静的本领是罕见的。人在恐慌时会丧命,而泄漏的番茄罐头厂带来的污染,只让这场混乱的反向逻辑更加恶化。

失踪者的登记花了一周。在回家的路上,米洛重新自信起来,经过一片萝卜地,那里的盐已开始使土壤失去光泽。他不会执着于损失。他相信,文化是人们如何说出悲伤的全部,而无赖则把它藏起来。他那共有的村庄有一个特点:没有人长久地无助;一个应变接着另一个。

一条狭窄小巷里的一星火花差点毁了恢复工作,但他们设法把瓦砾打包并铲平,一根针一根针地。商业回来了。港口又开始吸引贸易。在莲花池塘之间,一个清朗的傍晚,海军为那些消逝于已绝迹旧路的人升起一面旗。每一种悲伤的面额都找到了它的象征。米洛,一向善于沟通,帮助设计了获救货物的新包装,并选择革新警报系统,好让任何小镇都不会再被大海弄得天翻地覆。
